author MarcoFalke <*~=`'#}+{/-|&$^_@721217.xyz> 1774437944 +0100
committer fanquake <fanquake@gmail.com> 1775006820 +0800

fuzz: Remove unused g_setup pointers

These are unused and removing them avoids clang warnings like:

src/test/fuzz/deserialize.cpp:42:26: error: variable g_setup set but not used [-Werror,-Wunused-but-set-variable]

Github-Pull: #34918
Rebased-From: fabbfec3b00c138a28034a4f5594305d2220b9bb

verification.payload:
tree f638133b36e591d2aaa2a914ccfb3053b6c5145f
parent 98551a0efdccd27d9e4c1e45a9b3d02f02aaf8bf
author Ava Chow <github@achow101.com> 1774657862 -0700
committer fanquake <fanquake@gmail.com> 1775006820 +0800

fuzz: Use CAmount for storing best_waste

Waste is a CAmount, which is an int64_t. This will overflow an int, so
`best_waste` should also be a `CAmount`.

Github-Pull: #34939
Rebased-From: 890a09b1e49925315a5636f29cc5af36928fe092

verification.payload:
tree c894c6c88894d43dbb953bd1847240e861a070aa
parent 56cdeab8b548d6ab09660e239b6d1c12177340eb
author Alexander Wiederin <alex@wiederin.xyz> 1775042634 +0200
committer fanquake <fanquake@gmail.com> 1775184026 +0800

kernel: Remove NONNULL annotation from destroy method

No other *_destroy function in the Kernel API carries this annotation.
Following the convention set by free(), destroy functions should accept
null pointers.

Github-Pull: #34982
Rebased-From: 75608547b469b72caa890c8b356d0d3284998701

verification.payload:
tree 89d2d093d63c93d8b961b1fb4966611c65b04ed0
parent f0e1014c396c80cc946beff719af04ebfd482f82
author Hennadii Stepanov <32963518+hebasto@users.noreply.github.com> 1774866583 +0100
committer fanquake <fanquake@gmail.com> 1775184350 +0800

guix: Clean up module list in manifest

These modules are no longer in use:
1. `cmake-build-system` since bitcoin/bitcoin#34550.
2. `gnu-build-system` since bitcoin/bitcoin#34627.
3. `download` since bitcoin/bitcoin#34550.
4. `cc-for-target` from `utils` since bitcoin/bitcoin#34627.

Github-Pull: #34944
Rebased-From: 325f743eedd25be268f74b9cf91198f50d2b3cf0

events.body: What's the rationale for backporting https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34982? It's not part of any release binaries and the interface is experimental?
